data.title: Nvidia's Latest AI Deal Gets a Nod from Wall Street. Top Analysts Weigh In
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) made a low-key move in AI this week, and Wall Street took notice. After Nvidia announced a licensing deal with private AI startup Groq, two top analysts from BofA and Robert W. Baird reaffirmed their bullish views, saying the deal supports Nvidia's growing focus on AI inference.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The deal is a "non-exclusive" license that lets Nvidia access and use Groq's AI inference technology. Groq will continue to run independently, and media reports suggest the deal could be worth up to $20 billion. As part of the deal, Groq CEO Jonathan Ross will join Nvidia. Ross previously worked at Google (GOOGL) and was involved in the early development of Google's Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) program.

Top Analysts Reaffirm Ratings After Groq News

Following the announcement, Tristan Gerra, a 5-star analyst at Baird, reiterated an Outperform rating on Nvidia and maintained his $275 price target. Gerra said the Groq deal aligns well with Nvidia's strategy as AI workloads increasingly shift toward inference, where models are run and used in real-world settings rather than just trained.

He also noted that Nvidia has been building a large internal team focused on custom ASIC development. In his view, Nvidia's strength in software — especially its CUDA platform and AI libraries — gives the company a clear edge. That software lead, he said, could help Groq's inference technology scale more easily and reach a wider set of customers over time.

In a separate note, Vivek Arya, a 5-star analyst at BofA Securities, also reiterated a Buy rating on Nvidia, calling the stock a "top sector pick." Arya said the Groq deal was a surprise move announced just before Christmas and involves a very different type of hardware. Groq's technology uses LPUs, or language processing units, which are built for AI inference, while Nvidia's GPUs are mainly used for AI training.

He added that the deal shows Nvidia is getting ready for a shift in AI demand toward inference. While working with a new type of hardware could make product plans and pricing harder to manage, Arya said the move gives Nvidia more flexibility and helps it offer customers more choice.

What Is a Good Price for NVDA?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on NVDA stock based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NVDA price target of $263.58 per share implies 39.8% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Will SoFi Technologies (SOFI) Stock Continue to Rise in 2026. Here's What Analysts Say
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI) stock has advanced about 9% over the past five trading sessions and has risen 78% year-to-date. The fintech company continues to impress investors with strong revenue and profitability growth, supported by continued expansion in its member base. While SOFI bulls see continued upside in the stock heading into 2026, other analysts remain cautious amid valuation concerns and macro uncertainty.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Neobank SoFi continues to bolster its position in the fintech space through solid execution, continued expansion of its member base, and innovative offerings. Notably, the company ended Q3 2025 with 12.6 million members and 18.6 million products, reflecting a 35% and 36% year-over-year growth, respectively. Also, SoFi is gaining from its focus on capital-light, fee-based revenue.

However, SOFI stock is currently trading at a forward price-to-earnings (non-GAAP) multiple of 75.01x, well above the sector average of 11.57x.

Analysts Are Divided on SOFI Stock

Recently, Needham analyst Kyle Peterson reiterated a Buy rating on SOFI stock with a price target of $36. The analyst adjusted his model to reflect SOFI's recent secondary offering of 54.5 million shares of its common stock for about $1.5 billion in gross proceeds. Peterson expects the offering to be relatively neutral to the company's earnings per share (EPS), as the additional proceeds can likely be used to drive asset growth and/or reduce funding costs. "We view the raise as opportunistic and believe it adds further flexibility for faster organic growth and/or accretive M&A in areas such as blockchain or AI applications," said Peterson.  

Additionally, Peterson views the lower deposit costs following recent rate cuts as a near-term tailwind for net interest margin (NIM) for SOFI and Axos Financial (AX), as these companies use their deposits for funding.

Meanwhile, JPMorgan analyst Reginald Smith increased his price target for SoFi Technologies stock to $31 from $28 and reaffirmed a Hold rating as part of his 2026 outlook for the fintech space. The firm sees a "soft-landing grind" next year, with slowing real growth for the sector due to a weakening labor market and the lagged impact of tariffs, partially offset by tax cuts.

Is SOFI a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on SoFi Technologies stock based on five Buys, seven Holds, and four Sell recommendations. The average SOFI stock price target of $27.50 indicates shares are fully priced at current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Is the Stock Market Open Today, the Day After Christmas?
data.description:
The markets are open today, Friday, December 26, the day after Christmas.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Keep track of all the stock market holidays with TipRanks' Stock Market Holidays calendar.

Will the Holiday Effect Impact the Markets This Week?

Understanding how the stock market might be affected by the holiday will help you invest well and beat the markets. Investors sometimes refer to the pre-holiday market rise and the post-holiday price drift as the "holiday effect."

The holiday effect is linked to investors' general optimism and confidence. The fact that customers tend to spend more money around the holidays is one explanation for the pre-holiday market surge. The share prices of retailers, in particular, may increase as a result.

As for the post-holiday price drift, one reason is that many traders and market analysts take time off work during the holidays to relax from their regular trading and investment research schedules. Those investors view the holidays as a time to unwind and refresh. Alternatively, some traders see the season as a chance to work harder and get some tactical advantages in the market, maybe by taking advantage of the period when many other traders take time off.

Read TipRanks News to learn about the Christmas holiday effect on the stock market. And remember to always use TipRanks to stay up-to-date on your stock research.
data.author: TipRanks Team

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 12/26/25 – U.S. Stock Futures Inch Lower after Christmas Holiday
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged lower on early Friday after markets reopened following the Christmas break. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.12%, 0.10%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 3:33 a.m. EST on December 26.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

U.S. stock markets were closed on Thursday for Christmas Day after an early close on Wednesday, keeping trading activity muted. Volumes are expected to remain light ahead of a shortened New Year's week. Earlier in the week, the S&P 500 closed at a record high, supported by stronger-than-expected U.S. economic growth in the third quarter. Other major indexes also extended their winning streak to four sessions, helped by big tech names such as Alphabet (GOOGL), Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Amazon (AMZN).

As markets return to normal trading on Friday, investors will seek more insights from economic releases. With volumes thin, trading could remain calm, though late December often brings the seasonal "Santa Claus rally."

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.15%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $58.45 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,515 per ounce on Wednesday, continuing to touch record highs.

Elsewhere, several European markets were closed for the Boxing Day holiday. Even though markets in Germany, Italy, and Spain are open, trading is low as many investors are away for the holiday period.

Asia-Pacific Markets Close Higher on Friday

Australia and Hong Kong markets were closed on Friday for the Boxing Day holiday, while the remaining Asia-Pacific markets closed higher.

The Shanghai Composite gained 0.10%, while the Shenzhen Component added 0.54%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei rose 0.68% amid strength in tech stocks, while the Topix advanced 0.15%.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Intel Stock Is Up 80%+ YTD, But Wall Street Remains Cautious Into 2026
data.description:
Chip company Intel (INTC) has had a strong run, with the stock up 80.3% year-to-date and 77% over the past year. The rally reflects rising optimism around Intel's long-term turnaround plan, particularly its push to rebuild manufacturing capacity and strengthen its AI roadmap. Even so, Wall Street remains cautious, with analysts seeing only about 5% upside from current levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why the Stock Has Moved Higher

To start, Intel's rebound has been driven more by improving sentiment than by a full recovery in results. The latest Q3 earnings report showed better cost control and early signs of stability after a difficult period, which helped ease investor concerns.

Beyond earnings, capital investment has played a key role. Investors have reacted positively to Intel's large spending on new chip plants in the U.S. and Europe, projects that are supported by government funding. Sentiment received another boost after Nvidia (NVDA) disclosed a $5 billion investment in Intel, a move widely seen as a vote of confidence in its manufacturing plans. While this investment does not change Intel's near-term business outlook, it reinforces the turnaround story and supports recent gains.

Adding to that, the broader AI rally has lifted interest across the semiconductor sector. Intel has benefited from this sector-wide momentum, even as it continues to play catch-up with leading AI chipmakers.

Where Caution Still Comes In

Despite the stock's rise, Intel's core challenges remain. In the latest quarter, profit margins showed improvement and free cash flow turned positive, helped by better execution. Still, heavy spending on new factories continues to weigh on its results and limits near-term flexibility. Also, most of the payoff from these investments is still several years away.

At the same time, Intel lags key rivals such as Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Nvidia in high-end AI chips. Intel has introduced new AI-focused products, but customer adoption has been slower than what investors have seen from these competitors. This gap in AI performance and demand is a key reason analysts remain cautious.

Finally, Intel's foundry business is another source of risk. The strategy is central to the company's future, but it requires tight cost control and on-time delivery. Any delays, cost overruns, or execution issues could delay profitability and weigh on investor confidence.

Is Intel Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Taking all of this into account, analyst sentiment remains mixed. The stock is no longer deeply discounted, yet earnings growth is still not strong enough to support a clear Buy call. As a result, many analysts prefer to wait for clearer proof that Intel's turnaround is delivering consistent results.

On TipRanks, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on five Buys, 20 Holds and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. The average INTC price target of $38.20 per share implies 5.64% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Thinking of Buying Nvidia Stock? Weigh These 2026 Risks First
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) remains one of the most important stocks in the U.S. market, supported by strong demand for AI data-center chips and its leadership in advanced computing. Even as talk of an AI bubble has grown louder, NVDA shares are still up more than 40% year-to-date. The company also posted record quarterly revenue of about $57 billion in its most recent results, driven by a sharp rise in data-center sales, highlighting its central role in AI infrastructure.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

But as the year draws to a close and investors look ahead to 2026, several risks could challenge Nvidia's near-term prospects.

1. Export Limits Continue to Weigh on Nvidia's China Business

Nvidia's H200 chip is one of the company's most advanced products and is used in high-end AI computing. Demand for the chip is strong, but sales to China face close review.

U.S. officials have recently approved limited shipments of H200 chips to China. At the same time, some lawmakers have raised concerns that the technology could be used for military-related AI applications. Those concerns increase the risk of tighter rules or delays in future approvals.

Looking ahead, wider export licenses could support demand if they are fully rolled out. For now, however, the policy outlook remains unclear. Because of that uncertainty, China continues to be a key risk for Nvidia heading into 2026.

2. Rising Competition Tests Nvidia's Market Lead

At the same time, Nvidia is facing rising competition across the AI chip market. While the company remains the clear leader in high-end GPUs, rivals are working to close the gap.

Chipmakers such as Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Intel (INTC) are investing heavily in their own AI processors. At the same time, major cloud providers are working on custom chips to cut their reliance on Nvidia. For example, Alphabet (GOOGL) continues to develop its in-house Tensor Processing Units, or TPUs, which are designed to handle AI workloads inside Google's data centers. If more cloud customers move workloads to in-house silicon over time, Nvidia could see slower growth, even as overall AI demand remains strong.

Meanwhile, Nvidia has also adjusted its own strategy. The company recently pulled back plans to compete directly in cloud services through its DGX Cloud platform. Instead of going head-to-head with cloud leaders like Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), and Microsoft (MSFT), Nvidia is refocusing DGX Cloud on supporting internal AI development and partners.

The decision lowers execution risk but also highlights how crowded the AI infrastructure market has become. As more companies invest in chips and platforms, Nvidia's ability to protect market share and pricing will be closely watched heading into 2026.

3. High Valuation Raises the Stakes for 2026

Nvidia's stock trades at elevated levels compared with its history, leaving little room for disappointment. NVDA currently trades at a price-to-earnings multiple of roughly 46x, well above the semiconductor sector median near the mid-20s and higher than peers such as Alphabet, Amazon, and Microsoft, which trade closer to the low-30s range.

With so much optimism already priced in, even a modest slowdown in revenue growth, margin expansion, or guidance could trigger a sharp reaction in the stock. 

Is NVDA a Good Stock to Buy? 

Despite the above-mentioned risks, many Wall Street analysts remain positive on Nvidia's long-term outlook. Most continue to rate the stock a Buy and have kept high price targets, citing strong data-center demand and Nvidia's central role in AI infrastructure. 

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell recommendation. The average NVDA stock price target of $263.58 indicates about 40% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Analysts See 2026 as a Breakout Year for Microsoft Stock (MSFT)
data.description:
As we head into 2026, the technology sector sits at a pivotal moment. The artificial intelligence (AI) revolution is clearly underway, yet investors remain divided. On one hand, this represents a genuine Fourth Industrial Revolution, with the U.S. firmly back in the lead on foundational technology for the first time in decades. On the other hand, the sheer scale of capital investment required to support enterprise and consumer AI has made some investors cautious.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

I believe Microsoft (MSFT) sits directly at the center of this transition—and 2026 is shaping up to be its sweet spot. As enterprises move from experimentation to full-scale AI deployment, Microsoft's combination of Azure, Copilot, and its massive installed base positions the company to be one of the biggest beneficiaries of the next phase of AI adoption. For that reason, I remain bullish on Microsoft and view it as one of the most compelling large-cap technology opportunities heading into 2026.

A Strong Year, With More to Come

Microsoft shares are up roughly 16% this year, broadly in line with the S&P 500 (SPX). While that performance may appear "average" on the surface, I believe it masks what is coming next. AI adoption across enterprises remains in its early stages. Many CIOs are only now formalizing long-term AI strategies. That shift—from pilots to production—is where Microsoft's platform strength becomes most visible.
data.author: Davit Kirakosyan

data.title: What's Next for UnitedHealth Stock (UNH) After the Recent Rebound? Analysts Weigh In
data.description:
Health insurer UnitedHealth Group (UNH) saw its shares rebound after a recent slide. The stock closed higher on Wednesday, ending a seven-session losing streak. The pullback came as investors reacted to regulatory reviews, Medicare Advantage billing scrutiny, and broader weakness across the healthcare sector. While these near-term issues remain in focus, most Wall Street analysts continue to take a constructive view on UnitedHealth's longer-term outlook, pointing to plans to improve margins and steady growth.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts Remain Positive on UnitedHealth 

Recently, J.P. Morgan analyst Lisa Gill has reiterated a Buy rating on UNH, saying the stock carries lower risk than many other managed care names. She expects earnings to improve as the company benefits from better pricing in its insurance business and gradual improvement at Optum, helping growth recover over time.

Gill also views UnitedHealth as one of the steadier names in managed care, pointing to its size and broad mix of businesses. She believes this gives the company more room to manage Medicare Advantage while working through challenges in Medicaid and ACA plans.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley believes UnitedHealth is taking firm steps to improve margins, even if that comes at the cost of slower membership growth. The firm pointed to benefit cuts, tighter pricing, and plans to shrink Medicare Advantage enrollment in 2026. Morgan Stanley expects Medicare Advantage margins to rise to about 2%–3% in 2026 and believes that a better rate outlook could support this recovery. 

Is UNH a Good Stock to Buy? 

Despite near-term challenges, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on UnitedHealth stock based on 17 Buys, three Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average UNH stock price target of $392.32 indicates about 20% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: PLTR vs. BBAI: Wall Street Weighs Which AI Stock Has More Upside in 2026
data.description:
As AI stocks continue to grab attention, investors are also looking at companies tied to government, defense, and enterprise AI spending. In this space, Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and BigBear.ai (BBAI) have emerged as two closely watched names, each offering a different way to gain exposure to AI adoption. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we look at which stock analysts believe has more upside heading into 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Is Palantir Stock a Buy or Sell Now?

Palantir has become one of the most visible AI stocks over the past year, supported by steady demand from government agencies and rising interest from commercial customers. Shares have surged 158% year-to-date, driven in part by growing adoption of the company's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP). At the same time, long-term government contracts continue to provide a stable revenue base.

Analysts highlight improving margins, rising free cash flow, and expanding commercial revenue as key positives. Still, Wall Street remains cautious at current levels. Valuation is the main concern, with Palantir trading at a forward P/E of 268x, far above the sector average of about 25, suggesting much of the near-term growth may already be priced in.

That said, some analysts remain bullish on Palantir's longer-term outlook. For instance, top Bank of America analyst Mariana Perez Mora reiterated a Buy rating on Palantir and set a $255 price target, well above current levels. The analyst said Palantir's revenue growth shows no signs of slowing, fueled by strong demand from enterprise customers and expanding government contracts.

Is BigBear.ai a Good Stock to Buy Ahead of 2026?

BigBear.ai is a smaller and more speculative AI name, with a focus on defense, intelligence, and national security work. The company has benefited from rising interest in AI-driven defense tools as governments invest more in data analysis and decision-support systems. The stock has gained 49% year-to-date.

While revenue growth has improved, profitability remains a challenge. In the most recent quarter, BigBear.ai reported a net loss of $0.03 per share, narrower than the expected loss of $0.07. Adjusted EBITDA was negative $9.4 million, reflecting pressure from higher operating and SG&A costs.

From a valuation standpoint, BBAI trades at a price-to-sales multiple of about 13x, well above the sector median and its own historical average. However, it still trades below larger peers such as C3.ai (AI) and Palantir.

Wall Street analysts remain moderately bullish on BigBear.ai. They remain concerned about the execution risk due to the company's size and reliance on large government contracts, but believe that continued demand for defense-related AI work could support growth if the company executes well.

PLTR vs. BBAI: Which AI Stock Do Analysts Prefer?

Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, analysts currently show a more favorable view on BigBear.ai, which carries a Moderate Buy rating and an average price target of $6.50, implying about 8% upside from current levels. Meanwhile, Palantir is rated Hold, with an average price target of $187.87, suggesting around 3% downside.

While both stocks have delivered strong gains this year — BigBear.ai up nearly 49% and Palantir up about 158% — Wall Street appears more cautious at current prices, especially after the sharp rallies.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
